%% Table 1: Main Results
\begin{table}[t]
  \centering
  \small
  \caption{Main results on binary traffic action prediction. Val Acc is validation accuracy. HE-friendly protocol uses polynomial-approximated activations compatible with homomorphic encryption.}
  \label{tab:main_results}
  \begin{tabular}{lccc}
    \toprule
    \textbf{Method} & \textbf{Val Acc (\%)} & \textbf{$\Delta$ Local (pp)} & \textbf{Notes} \\
    \midrule
    \textbf{HEIR Cooperative (HE-friendly)} & \textbf{84.2} & \textbf{+3.3} & \textbf{Primary claim} \\
    HEIR Cooperative (plaintext) & 85.0 & +4.2 & Upper bound \\
    Local model (no cooperation) & 80.8 & — & Non-coop baseline \\
    Max-pressure heuristic & 77.5 & -3.3 & Traditional baseline \\
    Fixed-time control & 59.2 & -21.7 & Traditional baseline \\
    \bottomrule
  \end{tabular}
  \vspace{2pt}
  \footnotesize{\textit{HE overhead vs plaintext: 0.8 pp. Gain over best traditional baseline: +6.7 pp.}}
\end{table}

%% Table 2: Seed Sweep
\begin{table}[t]
  \centering
  \small
  \caption{Seed sweep results across 3 random seeds. Mean cooperative gain: +5.0 pp.}
  \label{tab:seed_sweep}
  \begin{tabular}{lcc}
    \toprule
    \textbf{Seed} & \textbf{HEIR Coop HE (\%)} & \textbf{Local (\%)} \\
    \midrule
    7 & 84.2 & 80.8 \\
    13 & 92.5 & 86.7 \\
    23 & 89.2 & 83.3 \\
    \textbf{Mean $\pm$ Std} & \textbf{88.6 $\pm$ 0.034} & \textbf{83.6 $\pm$ 0.024} \\
    \bottomrule
  \end{tabular}
\end{table}

%% Table 3: Ablation Study
\begin{table}[t]
  \centering
  \small
  \caption{Ablation study. Each row removes one feature group from the full HEIR cooperative model. Robustness row reports held-out adversarial evaluation.}
  \label{tab:ablation}
  \begin{tabular}{lcc}
    \toprule
    \textbf{Variant} & \textbf{Val Acc (\%)} & \textbf{$\Delta$ Full HEIR (pp)} \\
    \midrule
    \textbf{Full HEIR (HE-friendly)} & \textbf{84.2} & \textbf{—} \\
    w/o directional features & 80.8 & −3.3 \\
    w/o neighbor features & 80.8 & −3.3 \\
    w/o interaction features & 85.0 & +0.8 \\
    Local model (no cooperation) & 80.8 & −3.3 \\
    Robustness eval (HEIR coop) & 83.3 & — (+2.5 vs local) \\
    \bottomrule
  \end{tabular}
\end{table}

%% Table 4: Action-4 Multiclass (secondary)
\begin{table}[t]
  \centering
  \small
  \caption{Action-4 multiclass prediction results (one-vs-rest, OvR). Overall val accuracy: 68.1\%. Binary story is the primary claim; this is exploratory.}
  \label{tab:action4}
  \begin{tabular}{lcccc}
    \toprule
    \textbf{Class} & \textbf{Precision} & \textbf{Recall} & \textbf{F1} & \textbf{Support} \\
    \midrule
    Action 0 & 0.767 & 0.793 & 0.780 & 87 \\
    Action 1 & 0.516 & 0.615 & 0.561 & 26 \\
    Action 2 & 0.688 & 0.579 & 0.629 & 19 \\
    Action 3 & 0.286 & 0.167 & 0.211 & 12 \\
    \textbf{Macro avg} & \textbf{—} & \textbf{—} & \textbf{0.545} & \textbf{144} \\
    \bottomrule
  \end{tabular}
  \vspace{2pt}
  \footnotesize{\textit{Class imbalance is significant (Action 0 dominates). See action4\_metrics.json for full confusion matrix.}}
\end{table}
